context.Canceled) {\n727: \t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n728: \t}\n729: \n730: \treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\n731: }\n732: \n733: func (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) (api *API) labelValues(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n811: \tctx := r.Context()\n812: \tname := route.Param(ctx, \"name\")\n813: \n814: \tif strings.HasPrefix(name, \"U__\") {\n815: timestamp used for the start of optional time ranges.\n910: \t// Exposed to let downstream projects reference it.\n911: \t//\n912: \t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n913: \t// but it was set to a higher value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n914: \t// The value is still low enough for practical purposes, so we don't GlobalURLOptions struct {\n1069: \tListenAddress string\n1070: \tHost          string\n1071: \tScheme        string\n1072: }\n1073: \n1074: // sanitizeSplitHostPort acts like net.SplitHostPort.\n1075: // Additionally, if there is no port in the host passed as input, we return the\n1076: // original host, making sure that IPv6 addresses are not surrounded by square\n1077: // brackets.\n1078: func sanitizeSplitHostPort(input string) (string, string, error) {\n1079: \thost, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(input)\n1080: \tif err != nil && strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), \"missing port in address\") {\n1081: \t\tvar errWithPort error\n1082: \t\thost, _, errWithPort = net.SplitHostPort(input + \":80\")\n1083: \t\tif errWithPort == nil {\n1084: \t\t\terr = nil\n1085: \t\t}\n1086: \t}\n1087: \treturn host, port, err\n1088: }\n1089: \n1090: func getGlobalURL(u *url.URL, opts GlobalURLOptions) (*url.URL, error) {\n1091: \thost, port, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(u.Host)\n1092: \tif err != nil {\n1093: \t\treturn u, err\n1094: \t}\n1095: \n1096: \tfor _, lhr := range LocalhostRepresentations {\n1097: \t\tif host == lhr {\n1098: \t\t\t_, ownPort, err := net.SplitHostPort(opts.ListenAddress)\n1099: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n1100: \t\t\t\treturn u, err\n1101: \t\t\t}\n1102: \n1103: \t\t\tif port == ownPort {\n1104: \t\t\t\t// Only in the case where the target is on localhost and its port is\n1105: \t\t\t\t// the same as the one we're listening on, we know for sure that\n1106: \t\t\t\t// we're monitoring our own process and that we need to change the\n1107: \t\t\t\t// scheme, hostname, and port to the externally reachable ones as\n1108: \t\t\t\t// well. We shouldn't need to touch the path at all, since if a\n1109: \t\t\t\t// path prefix is defined, the path under which we scrape ourselves\n1110: \t\t\t\t// should already contain the prefix.\n1111: \t\t\t\tu.Scheme = opts.Scheme\n1112: \t\t\t\tu.Host = opts.Host\n1113: \t\t\t} else {\n1114: \t\t\t\t// Otherwise, we only know that localhost is not reachable\n1115: \t\t\t\t// externally, so we replace only the hostname by the one in the\n1116: \t\t\t\t// external URL. It could be the wrong hostname for the service on\n1117: \t\t\t\t// this port, but it's still the best possible guess.\n1118: \t\t\t\thost, _, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(opts.Host)\n1119: \t\t\t\tif \t\t\t\t}\n1126: \t\t\t}\n1127: \t\t\tbreak\n1128: \t\t}\n1129: \t}\n1130: \n1131: \treturn u, nil\n1132: }\n1133: \n1134: func (api *API) scrapePools(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1135: \tnames `json:\"keepFiringSince,omitempty\"`\n1341: \tValue           string        `json:\"value\"`\n1342: }\n1343: \n1344: func (api *API) alerts(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n1345: \talertingRules := \t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1423: \t\t\t\t}\n1424: \n1425: \t\t\t\tif !ok {\n1426: \t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n1427: \t\t\t\t\tmetrics[md.MetricFamily] = ms\n1428: \t\t\t\t}\n1429: \t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n1430: \t\t\t}\n1431: \t\t}\n1432: \t}\n1433: \n1434: \t// Put the elements from the pseudo-set into a slice for marshaling.\n1435: \tres := []metadata.Metadata{}\n1442: \t\tfor metadata := range set {\n1443: \t\t\ts = append(s, metadata)\n1444: \t\t}\n1445: \t\tres[name] = s\n1446: \t}\n1447: \n1448: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1449: }\n1450: \n1451: // RuleDiscovery has info for all rules.\n1452: type RuleDiscovery struct {\n1453: \tRuleGroups     []*RuleGroup `json:\"groups\"`\n1454: \tGroupNextToken string       `json:\"groupNextToken,omitempty\"`\n1455: }\n1456: \n1457: // RuleGroup has info for rules which are part of a group.\n1458: type RuleGroup struct {\n1459: \tName string `json:\"name\"`\n1460: \tFile {\n1504: \tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n1505: \t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n1506: \t}\n1507: \n1508: \tqueryFormToSet := func(values []string) map[string]struct{} {\n1509: \t\tset := make(map[string]struct{}, len(values))\n1510: \t\tfor _, v := range values {\n1511: \t\t\tset[v] = struct{}{}\n1512: \t\t}\n1513: \t\treturn set\n1514: \t}\n1515: \n1516: \trnSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_name[]\"])\n1517: \trgSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_group[]\"])\n1518: \tfSet := := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\"))\n1528: \n1529: \tif typ != \"\" && typ != \"alert\" && typ != \"record\" {\n1530: \t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"not supported value %q\", typ), \"type\")\n1531: \t}\n1532: \n1533: \treturnAlerts := typ == \"\" || typ == \"alert\"\n1534: \treturnRecording := typ == \"\" || typ == \"record\"\n1535: \n1536: \texcludeAlerts, err := parseExcludeAlerts(r)\n1537: \tif err != nil {\n1538: \t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"exclude_alerts\")\n1539: \t}\n1540: \n1541: \tmaxGroups, nextToken, parseErr := parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r)\n1542: \tif parseErr != nil {\n1543: \t\treturn *parseErr\n1544: \t}\n1545: \n1546: \trgs := make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))\n1547: \n1548: \tfoundToken := false\n1549: \n1550: \tfor _, grp := range ruleGroups {\n1551: \t\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n1552: \t\t\tif nextToken != getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name()) {\n1553: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1554: \t\t\t}\n1555: \t\t\tfoundToken = true\n1556: \t\t}\n1557: \n1558: \t\tif len(rgSet) > 0 {\n1559: \t\t\tif _, ok := rgSet[grp.Name()]; !ok {\n1560: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1561: \t\t\t}\n1562: \t\t}\n1563: \n1564: \t\tif len(fSet) > 0 {\n1565: \t\t\tif _, ok := fSet[grp.File()]; !ok {\n1566: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n1567: \t\t\t}\n1568: \t\t}\n1569: \n1570: grp.Limit(),\n1575: \t\t\tRules:          []Rule{},\n1576: \t\t\tEvaluationTime: grp.GetEvaluationTime().Seconds(),\n1577: \t\t\tLastEvaluation: grp.GetLastEvaluation(),\n1578: \t\t}\n1579: \n1580: \t\tfor _, rr := range grp.Rules(matcherSets...) {\n1581: \t\t\tvar enrichedRule Rule\n1582: \n1583: \t\t\tif len(rnSet) > 0 {\n1584: \t\t\t\tif _, ok := rnSet[rr.Name()]; !ok {\n1585: \t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1586: \t\t\t\t}\n1587: \t\t\t}\n1588: \n1589: \t\t\tlastError := \"\"\n1590: \t\t\tif rr.LastError() != nil {\n1591: \t\t\t\tlastError = rr.LastError().Error()\n1592: \t\t\t}\n1593: \t\t\tswitch rule := rr.(type) {\n1594: \t\t\tcase *rules.AlertingRule:\n1595: \t\t\t\tif !returnAlerts {\n1596: \t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1597: \t\t\t\t}\n1598: \t\t\t\tvar activeAlerts rule.Annotations(),\n1611: \t\t\t\t\tAlerts:         activeAlerts,\n1612: \t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n1613: \t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n1614: \t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: \t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n1630: \t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n1631: \t\t\t\t\tType:           \"recording\",\n1632: \t\t\t\t}\n1633: \t\t\tdefault:\n1634: \t\t\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"failed to assert type of rule '%v'\", rule.Name())\n1635: \t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n1636: \t\t\t}\n1637: \n1638: \t\t\tif enrichedRule != nil {\n1639: \t\t\t\tapiRuleGroup.Rules = append(apiRuleGroup.Rules, enrichedRule)\n1640: \t\t\t}\n1641: \t\t}\n1642: \n1643: \t\t// If the rule group response has no rules, skip it - this means we filtered all the rules of this group.\n1644: \t\tif len(apiRuleGroup.Rules) > 0 {\n1645: \t\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && len(rgs) == int(maxGroups) {\n1646: \t\t\t\t// We've reached the capacity of our page plus one. That means that for sure there will be at least one\n1647: \t\t\t\t// rule group in a subsequent request. Therefore a next token is required.\n1648: \t\t\t\tres.GroupNextToken = getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name())\n1649: \t\t\t\tbreak\n1650: \t\t\t}\n1651: \t\t\trgs = append(rgs, groups changed?\", nextToken), \"group_next_token\")\n1657: \t}\n1658: \n1659: \tres.RuleGroups = rgs\n1660: \treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n1661: }\n1662: \n1663: func parseExcludeAlerts(r *http.Request) (bool, error) {\n1664: \texcludeAlertsParam := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"exclude_alerts\"))\n1665: \n1666: \tif excludeAlertsParam == \"\" {\n1667: \t\treturn false, nil\n1668: \t}\n1669: \n1670: \texcludeAlerts, err := strconv.ParseBool(excludeAlertsParam)\n1671: \tif err != nil {\n1672: \t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"error converting hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n1713: }\n1714: \n1715: type prometheusConfig struct {\n1716: \tYAML string `json:\"yaml\"`\n1717: }\n1718: \n1719: func (api *API) serveRuntimeInfo(*http.Request) http.StatusBadRequest)\n1050: \t\t\treturn\n1051: \t\t}\n1052: \t\tresp := prompb.ReadResponse{\n1053: \t\t\tResults: make([]*prompb.QueryResult, len(req.Queries)),\n1054: \t\t}\n1055: \t\tfor i, \t\t\t\treturn\n1060: \t\t\t}\n1061: \n1062: \t\t\tvar hints *storage.SelectHints\n1063: \t\t\tif query.Hints != nil {\n1064: \t\t\t\thints = &storage.SelectHints{\n1065: \t\t\t\t\tStart: query.Hints.StartMs,\n1066: \t\t\t\t\tEnd:   query.Hints.EndMs,\n1067: \t\t\t\t\tStep:  query.Hints.StepMs,\n1068: \t\t\t\t\tFunc:  query.Hints.Func,\n1069: \t\t\t\t}\n1070: \t\t\t}\n1071: \n1072: \t\t\tquerier, err := s.Querier(query.StartTimestampMs, query.EndTimestampMs)\n1073: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n1074: \t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1075: \t\t\t\treturn\n1076: \t\t\t}\n1077: \t\t\tdefer querier.Close()\n1078: \n1079: \t\t\tset := querier.Select(r.Context(), false, hints, matchers...)\n1080: \t\t\tresp.Results[i], _, err = remote.ToQueryResult(set, 1e6)\n1081: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n1082: \t\t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1083: \t\t\t\treturn\n1084: \t\t\t}\n1085: \t\t}\n1086: \n1087: \t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/x-protobuf\")\n1088: \t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"snappy\")\n1089: \n1090: \t\tif err := remote.EncodeReadResponse(&resp, w); err != nil {\n1091: \t\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1092: \t\t\treturn\n1093: \t\t}\n1094: \t})\n1095: \n1096: \treturn httptest.NewServer(handler)\n1097: }\n1098: \n1099: Let me compile my findings into a final report.\n\n## Summary of Findings: Prometheus API v1 Web Endpoints\n\nBased on my search of the `github.com/sg-evals/prometheus--ba14bc4` repository, here are the key findings regarding API endpoint patterns, request/response handling, and Alertmanager integration:\n\n### 1. **API Endpoint Structure and Registration**\n\nThe Prometheus API v1 uses a route-based pattern for endpoint registration in `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go`. Key patterns:\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 371-459)\n\n```go\n// Register the API's endpoints in the given router.\nfunc (api *API) Register(r *route.Router) {\n  // Wrapping function for all endpoints\n  wrap := func(f apiFunc) http.HandlerFunc { ... }\n  \n  // Example endpoint registrations:\n  r.Get(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n  r.Post(\"/query\", wrapAgent(api.query))\n  r.Get(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n  r.Post(\"/query_range\", wrapAgent(api.queryRange))\n  \n  // Admin endpoints using POST/PUT\n  r.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/delete_series\", wrapAgent(api.deleteSeries))\n  r.Post(\"/admin/tsdb/clean_tombstones\", wrapAgent(api.cleanTombstones))\n  r.Put(\"/admin/tsdb/snapshot\", wrapAgent(api.snapshot))\n  \n  // Other handlers (remote read/write)\n  r.Post(\"/read\", api.ready(api.remoteRead))\n  r.Post(\"/write\", api.ready(api.remoteWrite))\n}\n```\n\n### 2. **HTTP Method Handling Pattern**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api_test.go` (lines 3676-3694)\n\nThe API supports both GET and POST for certain endpoints. POST is used with form-encoded data:\n\n```go\n// Query endpoints support both methods\nmethods := func(f apiFunc) []string {\n  fp := reflect.ValueOf(f).Pointer()\n  if fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.query).Pointer() || \n     fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.queryRange).Pointer() || \n     fp == reflect.ValueOf(api.series).Pointer() {\n    return []string{http.MethodGet, http.MethodPost}\n  }\n  return []string{http.MethodGet}\n}\n\n// For POST requests, parameters are URL-encoded in the body\nrequest := func(m string, q url.Values) (*http.Request, error) {\n  if m == http.MethodPost {\n    r, err := http.NewRequest(m, \"http://example.com\", \n      strings.NewReader(q.Encode()))\n    r.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\")\n    r.RemoteAddr = \"127.0.0.1:20201\"\n    return r, err\n  }\n  // GET uses query string\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Response Format Structure**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 184-192, 2005-2083)\n\nAll API responses follow a consistent JSON structure with the `Response` type:\n\n```go\n// Response contains a response to a HTTP API request.\ntype Response struct {\n  Status    status   `json:\"status\"`\n  Data      any      `json:\"data,omitempty\"`\n  ErrorType string   `json:\"errorType,omitempty\"`\n  Error     string   `json:\"error,omitempty\"`\n  Warnings  []string `json:\"warnings,omitempty\"`\n  Infos     []string `json:\"infos,omitempty\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Success Response Function**:\n```go\nfunc (api *API) respond(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request, \n  data any, warnings annotations.Annotations, query string) {\n  statusMessage := statusSuccess\n  warn, info := warnings.AsStrings(query, 10, 10)\n  \n  resp := &Response{\n    Status:   statusMessage,\n    Data:     data,\n    Warnings: warn,\n    Infos:    info,\n  }\n  \n  // Encode based on content negotiation (JSON by default)\n  w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", codec.ContentType().String())\n  w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n  w.Write(encodedResponse)\n}\n```\n\n**Error Response Function**:\n```go\nfunc (api *API) respondError(w http.ResponseWriter, apiErr *apiError, data any) {\n  resp := &Response{\n    Status:    statusError,\n    ErrorType: apiErr.typ.str,\n    Error:     apiErr.err.Error(),\n    Data:      data,\n  }\n  \n  // Error codes mapped to HTTP status\n  w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n  w.WriteHeader(getDefaultErrorCode(apiErr.typ))\n  w.Write(encodedResponse)\n}\n```\n\nError type to HTTP status mapping:\n- `errorBadData` → 400 Bad Request\n- `errorExec` → 422 Unprocessable Entity\n- `errorTimeout` → 503 Service Unavailable\n- `errorInternal` → 500 Internal Server Error\n- `errorNotFound` → 404 Not Found\n\n### 4. **apiFunc Pattern for Endpoint Handlers**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 194-201)\n\nAll endpoint handlers follow a common function type:\n\n```go\ntype apiFuncResult struct {\n  data      any\n  err       *apiError\n  warnings  annotations.Annotations\n  finalizer func()\n}\n\ntype apiFunc func(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult\n```\n\nExample endpoint implementation:\n```go\nfunc (api *API) deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n  if !api.enableAdmin {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // Process parameters and return result\n  return apiFuncResult{data, nil, warnings, finalizer}\n}\n```\n\n### 5. **Admin API Examples (POST/PUT Pattern)**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 1925-1950)\n\nThe `/admin/tsdb/*` endpoints show the pattern for POST/PUT operations:\n\n```go\nfunc (api *API) deleteSeries(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n  // 1. Enable check\n  if !api.enableAdmin {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorUnavailable, errors.New(\"admin APIs disabled\")}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // 2. Form parsing\n  if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // 3. Validate parameters\n  if len(r.Form[\"match[]\"]) == 0 {\n    return apiFuncResult{nil, \n      &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"no match[] parameter provided\")}, \n      nil, nil}\n  }\n  \n  // 4. Parse time parameters\n  start, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n  if err != nil {\n    return invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n  }\n  \n  // 5. Execute operation\n  for _, s := range r.Form[\"match[]\"] {\n    matchers, err := parser.ParseMetricSelector(s)\n    if err != nil {\n      return invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n    }\n    if err := api.db.Delete(r.Context(), timestamp.FromTime(start), \n      timestamp.FromTime(end), matchers...); err != nil {\n      // error handling\n    }\n  }\n  \n  // 6. Return success (no content)\n  return apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n```\n\n### 6. **Alertmanager Integration**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go` (lines 431, 2011-2016)\n\nPrometheus provides alertmanager discovery endpoint:\n\n```go\n// Endpoint registration\nr.Get(\"/alertmanagers\", wrapAgent(api.alertmanagers))\n\n// AlertmanagerDiscovery response type for API\ntype AlertmanagerDiscovery struct {\n  ActiveAlertmanagers []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"activeAlertmanagers\"`\n  DroppedAlertmanagers []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"droppedAlertmanagers\"`\n}\n\ntype AlertmanagerTarget struct {\n  URL string `json:\"url\"`\n}\n```\n\n**Important Finding**: The Prometheus API **does NOT have native silences endpoints**. Silences are managed by Alertmanager, not Prometheus. Prometheus only discovers and reports on Alertmanager instances.\n\n### 7. **NO Silences Endpoint in Prometheus API v1**\n\n**Critical Finding**: After thorough searching of the repository:\n- **No `/silences` endpoint** exists in `/web/api/v1/`\n- **No bulk silences creation** endpoint found\n- **No silence management** functionality in Prometheus\n\nThis is correct by design: **Alertmanager (not Prometheus) manages silences**. The relationship is:\n1. Prometheus generates alerts\n2. Prometheus sends alerts to Alertmanager\n3. Alertmanager manages silence policies (silence creation, updates)\n4. Prometheus only queries Alertmanager discovery\n\n### 8. **Test Pattern for Endpoint Testing**\n\n**File**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api_test.go` (lines 1099-1122, 3696-3700)\n\n```go\ntype test struct {\n  endpoint              apiFunc\n  params                map[string]string\n  query                 url.Values\n  response              any\n  responseLen           int\n  responseAsJSON        string\n  warningsCount         int\n  errType               errorType\n  sorter                func(any)\n}\n\n// Test execution\nfor i, test := range tests {\n  t.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"run %d %s %q\", i, describeAPIFunc(test.endpoint), \n    test.query.Encode()), func(t *testing.T) {\n    for _, method := range methods(test.endpoint) {\n      t.Run(method, func(t *testing.T) {\n        // Build request with correct params\n        req, _ := request(method, test.query)\n        \n        // Execute endpoint\n        result := test.endpoint(req)\n        \n        // Assert response\n        // ...\n      })\n    }\n  })\n}\n```\n\n### 9. **Key Files Referenced**\n\n1. **Main API implementation**: `/workspace/web/api/v1/api.go`\n   - nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) query(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\tts, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"time\", api.now())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"time\")\n\t}\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, api.now().Add(timeout))\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), ts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) formatQuery(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{expr.Pretty(0), nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) parseQuery(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{data: translateAST(expr), err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n}\n\nfunc extractQueryOpts(r *http.Request) (promql.QueryOpts, error) {\n\tvar duration time.Duration\n\n\tif strDuration := r.FormValue(\"lookback_delta\"); strDuration != \"\" {\n\t\tparsedDuration, err := parseDuration(strDuration)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing lookback delta duration: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tduration = parsedDuration\n\t}\n\n\treturn promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(r.FormValue(\"stats\") == \"all\", duration), nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryRange(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\tstart, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"start\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"end\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif end.Before(start) {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start time\"), \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tstep, err := parseDuration(r.FormValue(\"step\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\tif step <= 0 {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"zero or negative query resolution step widths are not accepted. Try a positive integer\"), \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\t// For safety, limit the number of returned points per timeseries.\n\t// This is sufficient for 60s resolution for a week or 1h resolution for a year.\n\tif end.Sub(start)/step > 11000 {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"exceeded maximum resolution of 11,000 points per timeseries. Try decreasing the query resolution (?step=XX)\")\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewRangeQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), start, end, step)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryExemplars(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif end.Before(start) {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start timestamp\")\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tselectors := parser.ExtractSelectors(expr)\n\tif len(selectors) < 1 {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\teq, err := api.ExemplarQueryable.ExemplarQuerier(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tres, err := eq.Select(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), selectors...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc returnAPIError(err error) *apiError {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar eqc promql.ErrQueryCanceled\n\tvar eqt promql.ErrQueryTimeout\n\tvar es promql.ErrStorage\n\tswitch {\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqc):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqt):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorTimeout, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &es):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorInternal, err}\n\t}\n\n\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tdefer q.Close()\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tnames    []string\n\t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t)\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tlabelNamesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n\n\t\tfor _, make([]string, 0, len(labelNamesSet))\n\t\tfor key := range labelNamesSet {\n\t\t\tnames = append(names, key)\n\t\t}\n\t\tslices.Sort(names)\n\t} else {\n\t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n\t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n\t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n\t\t}\n\t\tnames, warnings, err = q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif names == nil {\n\t\tnames = []string{}\n\t}\n\n\tif limit > 0 && len(names) > limit {\n\t\tnames = names[:limit]\n\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{names, nil, warnings, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelValues(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tvar callWarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t\tlabelValuesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n\t\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\n\t\t\tvals, callWarnings, err = matcherSets[0]\n\t\t}\n\t\tvals, warnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, hints, matchers...)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, closer}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif vals == nil {\n\t\t\tvals = []string{}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tslices.Sort(vals)\n\n\tif limit > 0 && len(vals) > limit {\n\t\tvals = vals[:limit]\n\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{vals, nil, warnings, closer}\n}\n\nvar (\n\t// MinTime is the default timestamp used for the start of optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a higher value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still low enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMinTime = time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000+62135596801, 0).UTC()\n\n\t// MaxTime is the default timestamp used for the end of optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects to reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a lower value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still high enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMaxTime = time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000-62135596801, 999999999).UTC()\n\n\tminTimeFormatted = MinTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\tmaxTimeFormatted = MaxTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n)\n\nfunc (api *API) series(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tif invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called {\n\t\tq.Close()\n\t}\n\n\thints := &storage.SelectHints{\n\t\tStart: timestamp.FromTime(start),\n\t\tEnd:   timestamp.FromTime(end),\n\t\tFunc:  \"series\", // There is no series function, this token is used for lookups that don't need samples.\n\t\tLimit: toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\tvar set storage.SeriesSet\n\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tvar sets []storage.SeriesSet\n\t\tfor _, mset := range matcherSets {\n\t\t\t// We need to sort this select results to merge (deduplicate) the series sets later.\n\t\t\ts := q.Select(ctx, true, hints, mset...)\n\t\t\tsets = append(sets, s)\n\t\t}\n\t\tset = storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(sets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// At this point at least one match exists.\n\t\tset = q.Select(ctx, false, hints, matcherSets[0]...)\n\t}\n\n\tmetrics := []labels.Labels{}\n\n\twarnings := set.Warnings()\n\n\ti := 1\n\tfor set.Next() {\n\t\tif i%checkContextEveryNIterations == 0 {\n\t\t\tif err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), warnings, closer}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\ti++\n\n\t\tmetrics = append(metrics, set.At().Labels())\n\n\t\tif limit > 0 && len(metrics) > limit {\n\t\t\tmetrics = metrics[:limit]\n\t\t\twarnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{metrics, nil, warnings, closer}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif set.Err() != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(set.Err()), warnings, closer}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{metrics, nil, warnings, closer}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) dropSeries(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, errors.New(\"not implemented\")}, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// Target has the information for one target.\ntype Target struct {\n\t// Labels before any processing.\n\tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n\t// Any labels that are added to this target and its metrics.\n\tLabels labels.Labels `json:\"labels\"`\n\n\tScrapePool string `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n\tScrapeURL  string `json:\"scrapeUrl\"`\n\tGlobalURL  string `json:\"globalUrl\"`\n\n\tLastError          string              `json:\"lastError\"`\n\tLastScrape         time.Time           `json:\"lastScrape\"`\n\tLastScrapeDuration float64             `json:\"lastScrapeDuration\"`\n\tHealth             scrape.TargetHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\n\tScrapeInterval string `json:\"scrapeInterval\"`\n\tScrapeTimeout  string `json:\"scrapeTimeout\"`\n}\n\ntype ScrapePoolsDiscovery struct {\n\tScrapePools []string `json:\"scrapePools\"`\n}\n\n// DroppedTarget has the information for one target that was dropped during relabelling.\ntype DroppedTarget struct {\n\t// Labels before any processing.\n\tDiscoveredLabels labels.Labels `json:\"discoveredLabels\"`\n\tScrapePool       string        `json:\"scrapePool\"`\n}\n\n// TargetDiscovery has all the active targets.\ntype TargetDiscovery struct {\n\tActiveTargets       []*Target        `json:\"activeTargets\"`\n\tDroppedTargets      []*DroppedTarget `json:\"droppedTargets\"`\n\tDroppedTargetCounts map[string]int   `json:\"droppedTargetCounts\"`\n}\n\n// GlobalURLOptions contains fields used for deriving the global URL for local targets.\ntype GlobalURLOptions struct {\n\tListenAddress string\n\tHost          string\n\tScheme        string\n}\n\n// sanitizeSplitHostPort acts like net.SplitHostPort.\n// Additionally, if there is no port in the host passed as input, we return the\n// original host, making sure that IPv6 addresses are not surrounded by square\n// brackets.\nfunc sanitizeSplitHostPort(input string) (string, string, error) {\n\thost, port, err := net.SplitHostPort(input)\n\tif err != nil && strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), \"missing port in address\") {\n\t\tvar errWithPort error\n\t\thost, _, errWithPort = net.SplitHostPort(input + \":80\")\n\t\tif errWithPort == nil {\n\t\t\terr = nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn host, port, err\n}\n\nfunc getGlobalURL(u *url.URL, opts GlobalURLOptions) (*url.URL, error) {\n\thost, port, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(u.Host)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn u, err\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, lhr := range LocalhostRepresentations {\n\t\tif host == lhr {\n\t\t\t_, ownPort, err := net.SplitHostPort(opts.ListenAddress)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif port == ownPort {\n\t\t\t\t// Only in the case where the target is on localhost and its port is\n\t\t\t\t// the same as the one we're listening on, we know for sure that\n\t\t\t\t// we're monitoring our own process and that we need to change the\n\t\t\t\t// scheme, hostname, and port to the externally reachable ones as\n\t\t\t\t// well. We shouldn't need to touch the path at all, since if a\n\t\t\t\t// path prefix is defined, the path under which we scrape ourselves\n\t\t\t\t// should already contain the prefix.\n\t\t\t\tu.Scheme = opts.Scheme\n\t\t\t\tu.Host = opts.Host\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Otherwise, we only know that localhost is not reachable\n\t\t\t\t// externally, so we replace only the hostname by the one in the\n\t\t\t\t// external URL. It could be the wrong hostname for the service on\n\t\t\t\t// this port, but it's still the best possible guess.\n\t\t\t\thost, _, err := sanitizeSplitHostPort(opts.Host)\n\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn u, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tu.Host = host\n\t\t\t\tif port != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tu.Host = net.JoinHostPort(u.Host, port)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn u, nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) scrapePools(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tnames := api.scrapePoolsRetriever(r.Context()).ScrapePools()\n\tsort.Strings(names)\n\tres := &ScrapePoolsDiscovery{ScrapePools: names}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{data: res, err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) targets(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tgetSortedPools := func(targets map[string][]*scrape.Target) ([]string, int) {\n\t\tvar n int\n\t\tpools := make([]string, 0, len(targets))\n\t\tfor p, t := range targets {\n\t\t\tpools = append(pools, p)\n\t\t\tn += len(t)\n\t\t}\n\t\tslices.Sort(pools)\n\t\treturn pools, n\n\t}\n\n\tscrapePool := r.URL.Query().Get(\"scrapePool\")\n\tstate := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"state\"))\n\tshowActive := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"active\"\n\tshowDropped := state == \"\" || state == \"any\" || state == \"dropped\"\n\tres := &TargetDiscovery{}\n\tbuilder := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\n\n\tif showActive {\n\t\ttargetsActive := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive()\n\t\tactivePools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsActive)\n\t\tres.ActiveTargets = make([]*Target, 0, numTargets)\n\n\t\tfor _, pool := range activePools {\n\t\t\tif scrapePool != \"\" && pool != scrapePool {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor _, target := range targetsActive[pool] {\n\t\t\t\tlastErrStr := \"\"\n\t\t\t\tlastErr := target.LastError()\n\t\t\t\tif lastErr != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tlastErrStr = lastErr.Error()\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tglobalURL, err := getGlobalURL(target.URL(), api.globalURLOptions)\n\n\t\t\t\tres.ActiveTargets = append(res.ActiveTargets, &Target{\n\t\t\t\t\tDiscoveredLabels: target.DiscoveredLabels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:           target.Labels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapePool:       pool,\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeURL:        target.URL().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tGlobalURL:        globalURL.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError: func() string {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase err == nil && lastErrStr == \"\":\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase err != nil:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s: %w\", lastErrStr, err).Error()\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn lastErrStr\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastScrape:         target.LastScrape(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastScrapeDuration: target.LastScrapeDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:             target.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeInterval:     target.GetValue(model.ScrapeIntervalLabel),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapeTimeout:      target.GetValue(model.ScrapeTimeoutLabel),\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tres.ActiveTargets = []*Target{}\n\t}\n\tif showDropped {\n\t\tres.DroppedTargetCounts = api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDroppedCounts()\n\n\t\ttargetsDropped := api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsDropped()\n\t\tdroppedPools, numTargets := getSortedPools(targetsDropped)\n\t\tres.DroppedTargets = make([]*DroppedTarget, 0, numTargets)\n\t\tfor _, pool := range droppedPools {\n\t\t\tif scrapePool != \"\" && pool != scrapePool {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor _, target := range targetsDropped[pool] {\n\t\t\t\tres.DroppedTargets = append(res.DroppedTargets, &DroppedTarget{\n\t\t\t\t\tDiscoveredLabels: target.DiscoveredLabels(builder),\n\t\t\t\t\tScrapePool:       pool,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tres.DroppedTargets = []*DroppedTarget{}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc matchLabels(lset labels.Labels, matchers []*labels.Matcher) bool {\n\tfor _, m := range matchers {\n\t\tif !m.Matches(lset.Get(m.Name)) {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) targetMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmatchTarget := r.FormValue(\"match_target\")\n\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n\tvar err error\n\tif matchTarget != \"\" {\n\t\tmatchers, err = parser.ParseMetricSelector(matchTarget)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match_target\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tbuilder := labels.NewBuilder(labels.EmptyLabels())\n\tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n\tres := []metricMetadata{}\n\tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n\t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n\t\t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttargetLabels := t.Labels(builder)\n\t\t\t// Filter targets that don't satisfy the label matchers.\n\t\t\tif matchTarget != \"\" && !matchLabels(targetLabels, matchers) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// If no metric is specified, get the full list for the target.\n\t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, md := range t.ListMetadata() {\n\t\t\t\t\tres = append(res, metricMetadata{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tTarget:       targetLabels,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tMetricFamily: md.MetricFamily,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tType:         md.Type,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tHelp:         md.Help,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tUnit:         md.Unit,\n\t\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Get metadata for the specified metric.\n\t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n\t\t\t\tres = append(res, metricMetadata{\n\t\t\t\t\tTarget: targetLabels,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:   md.Type,\n\t\t\t\t\tHelp:   md.Help,\n\t\t\t\t\tUnit:   md.Unit,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\ntype metricMetadata struct {\n\tTarget       labels.Labels    `json:\"target\"`\n\tMetricFamily string           `json:\"metric,omitempty\"`\n\tType         model.MetricType `json:\"type\"`\n\tHelp         string           `json:\"help\"`\n\tUnit         string           `json:\"unit\"`\n}\n\n// AlertmanagerDiscovery has all the active Alertmanagers.\ntype AlertmanagerDiscovery struct {\n\tActiveAlertmanagers  []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"activeAlertmanagers\"`\n\tDroppedAlertmanagers []*AlertmanagerTarget `json:\"droppedAlertmanagers\"`\n}\n\n// AlertmanagerTarget has info on one AM.\ntype AlertmanagerTarget struct {\n\tURL string `json:\"url\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) alertmanagers(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\turls := api.alertmanagerRetriever(r.Context()).Alertmanagers()\n\tdroppedURLS := api.alertmanagerRetriever(r.Context()).DroppedAlertmanagers()\n\tams := &AlertmanagerDiscovery{ActiveAlertmanagers: make([]*AlertmanagerTarget, len(urls)), DroppedAlertmanagers: make([]*AlertmanagerTarget, len(droppedURLS))}\n\tfor i, url := range urls {\n\t\tams.ActiveAlertmanagers[i] = &AlertmanagerTarget{URL: url.String()}\n\t}\n\tfor i, url := range droppedURLS {\n\t\tams.DroppedAlertmanagers[i] = &AlertmanagerTarget{URL: url.String()}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{ams, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// AlertDiscovery has info for all active alerts.\ntype AlertDiscovery struct {\n\tAlerts []*Alert `json:\"alerts\"`\n}\n\n// Alert has info for an alert.\ntype Alert struct {\n\tLabels          labels.Labels `json:\"labels\"`\n\tAnnotations     labels.Labels `json:\"annotations\"`\n\tState           string        `json:\"state\"`\n\tActiveAt        *time.Time    `json:\"activeAt,omitempty\"`\n\tKeepFiringSince *time.Time    `json:\"keepFiringSince,omitempty\"`\n\tValue           string        `json:\"value\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) alerts(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\talertingRules := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).AlertingRules()\n\talerts := []*Alert{}\n\n\tfor _, alertingRule := range alertingRules {\n\t\talerts = append(\n\t\t\talerts,\n\t\t\trulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(alertingRule.ActiveAlerts())...,\n\t\t)\n\t}\n\n\tres := &AlertDiscovery{Alerts: alerts}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rulesAlerts []*rules.Alert) []*Alert {\n\tapiAlerts := make([]*Alert, len(rulesAlerts))\n\tfor i, ruleAlert := range rulesAlerts {\n\t\tapiAlerts[i] = &Alert{\n\t\t\tLabels:      ruleAlert.Labels,\n\t\t\tAnnotations: ruleAlert.Annotations,\n\t\t\tState:       ruleAlert.State.String(),\n\t\t\tActiveAt:    &ruleAlert.ActiveAt,\n\t\t\tValue:       strconv.FormatFloat(ruleAlert.Value, 'e', -1, 64),\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tapiAlerts[i].KeepFiringSince = &ruleAlert.KeepFiringSince\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiAlerts\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) metricMetadata(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tmetrics := map[string]map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\n\tlimit := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tlimitPerMetric := -1\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit_per_metric\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limitPerMetric, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit_per_metric must be a number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tmetric := r.FormValue(\"metric\")\n\tfor _, tt := range api.targetRetriever(r.Context()).TargetsActive() {\n\t\tfor _, t := range tt {\n\t\t\tif metric == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, mm := range t.ListMetadata() {\n\t\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: mm.Type, Help: mm.Help, Unit: mm.Unit}\n\t\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[mm.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[mm.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif md, ok := t.GetMetadata(metric); ok {\n\t\t\t\tm := metadata.Metadata{Type: md.Type, Help: md.Help, Unit: md.Unit}\n\t\t\t\tms, ok := metrics[md.MetricFamily]\n\n\t\t\t\tif limitPerMetric > 0 && len(ms) >= limitPerMetric {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tms = map[metadata.Metadata]struct{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\tmetrics[md.MetricFamily] = ms\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tms[m] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Put the elements from the pseudo-set into a slice for marshaling.\n\tres := map[string][]metadata.Metadata{}\n\tfor name, set := range metrics {\n\t\tif limit >= 0 && len(res) >= limit {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\n\t\ts := []metadata.Metadata{}\n\t\tfor metadata := range set {\n\t\t\ts = append(s, metadata)\n\t\t}\n\t\tres[name] = s\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// RuleDiscovery has info for all rules.\ntype RuleDiscovery struct {\n\tRuleGroups     []*RuleGroup `json:\"groups\"`\n\tGroupNextToken string       `json:\"groupNextToken,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// RuleGroup has info for rules which are part of a group.\ntype RuleGroup struct {\n\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n\tFile string `json:\"file\"`\n\t// In order to preserve rule ordering, while exposing type (alerting or recording)\n\t// specific properties, both alerting and recording rules are exposed in the\n\t// same array.\n\tRules          []Rule    `json:\"rules\"`\n\tInterval       float64   `json:\"interval\"`\n\tLimit          int       `json:\"limit\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64   `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n}\n\ntype Rule any\n\ntype AlertingRule struct {\n\t// State can be \"pending\", \"firing\", \"inactive\".\n\tState          string           `json:\"state\"`\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tDuration       float64          `json:\"duration\"`\n\tKeepFiringFor  float64          `json:\"keepFiringFor\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels\"`\n\tAnnotations    labels.Labels    `json:\"annotations\"`\n\tAlerts         []*Alert         `json:\"alerts\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of an alertingRule is always \"alerting\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\ntype RecordingRule struct {\n\tName           string           `json:\"name\"`\n\tQuery          string           `json:\"query\"`\n\tLabels         labels.Labels    `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n\tHealth         rules.RuleHealth `json:\"health\"`\n\tLastError      string           `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n\tEvaluationTime float64          `json:\"evaluationTime\"`\n\tLastEvaluation time.Time        `json:\"lastEvaluation\"`\n\t// Type of a recordingRule is always \"recording\".\n\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) rules(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tqueryFormToSet := func(values []string) map[string]struct{} {\n\t\tset := make(map[string]struct{}, len(values))\n\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\t\tset[v] = struct{}{}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn set\n\t}\n\n\trnSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_name[]\"])\n\trgSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"rule_group[]\"])\n\tfSet := queryFormToSet(r.Form[\"file[]\"])\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\truleGroups := api.rulesRetriever(r.Context()).RuleGroups()\n\tres := &RuleDiscovery{RuleGroups: make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))}\n\ttyp := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\"))\n\n\tif typ != \"\" && typ != \"alert\" && typ != \"record\" {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"not supported value %q\", typ), \"type\")\n\t}\n\n\treturnAlerts := typ == \"\" || typ == \"alert\"\n\treturnRecording := typ == \"\" || typ == \"record\"\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := parseExcludeAlerts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"exclude_alerts\")\n\t}\n\n\tmaxGroups, nextToken, parseErr := parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r)\n\tif parseErr != nil {\n\t\treturn *parseErr\n\t}\n\n\trgs := make([]*RuleGroup, 0, len(ruleGroups))\n\n\tfoundToken := false\n\n\tfor _, grp := range ruleGroups {\n\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\t\tif nextToken != getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name()) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfoundToken = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(rgSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := rgSet[grp.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(fSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := fSet[grp.File()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tapiRuleGroup := &RuleGroup{\n\t\t\tName:           grp.Name(),\n\t\t\tFile:           grp.File(),\n\t\t\tInterval:       grp.Interval().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLimit:          grp.Limit(),\n\t\t\tRules:          []Rule{},\n\t\t\tEvaluationTime: grp.GetEvaluationTime().Seconds(),\n\t\t\tLastEvaluation: grp.GetLastEvaluation(),\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, rr := range grp.Rules(matcherSets...) {\n\t\t\tvar enrichedRule Rule\n\n\t\t\tif len(rnSet) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tif _, ok := rnSet[rr.Name()]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlastError := \"\"\n\t\t\tif rr.LastError() != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlastError = rr.LastError().Error()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tswitch rule := rr.(type) {\n\t\t\tcase *rules.AlertingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tvar activeAlerts []*Alert\n\t\t\t\tif !excludeAlerts {\n\t\t\t\t\tactiveAlerts = rulesAlertsToAPIAlerts(rule.ActiveAlerts())\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = AlertingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tState:          rule.State().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tDuration:       rule.HoldDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tKeepFiringFor:  rule.KeepFiringFor().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAnnotations:    rule.Annotations(),\n\t\t\t\t\tAlerts:         activeAlerts,\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"alerting\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tcase *rules.RecordingRule:\n\t\t\t\tif !returnRecording {\n\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tenrichedRule = RecordingRule{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:           rule.Name(),\n\t\t\t\t\tQuery:          rule.Query().String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLabels:         rule.Labels(),\n\t\t\t\t\tHealth:         rule.Health(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastError:      lastError,\n\t\t\t\t\tEvaluationTime: rule.GetEvaluationDuration().Seconds(),\n\t\t\t\t\tLastEvaluation: rule.GetEvaluationTimestamp(),\n\t\t\t\t\tType:           \"recording\",\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\terr := fmt.Errorf(\"failed to assert type of rule '%v'\", rule.Name())\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif enrichedRule != nil {\n\t\t\t\tapiRuleGroup.Rules = append(apiRuleGroup.Rules, enrichedRule)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If the rule group response has no rules, skip it - this means we filtered all the rules of this group.\n\t\tif len(apiRuleGroup.Rules) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif maxGroups > 0 && len(rgs) == int(maxGroups) {\n\t\t\t\t// We've reached the capacity of our page plus one. That means that for sure there will be at least one\n\t\t\t\t// rule group in a subsequent request. Therefore a next token is required.\n\t\t\t\tres.GroupNextToken = getRuleGroupNextToken(grp.File(), grp.Name())\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\trgs = append(rgs, apiRuleGroup)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif maxGroups > 0 && nextToken != \"\" && !foundToken {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"invalid group_next_token '%v'. were rule groups changed?\", nextToken), \"group_next_token\")\n\t}\n\n\tres.RuleGroups = rgs\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc parseExcludeAlerts(r *http.Request) (bool, error) {\n\texcludeAlertsParam := strings.ToLower(r.URL.Query().Get(\"exclude_alerts\"))\n\n\tif excludeAlertsParam == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\n\texcludeAlerts, err := strconv.ParseBool(excludeAlertsParam)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"error converting exclude_alerts: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\treturn excludeAlerts, nil\n}\n\nfunc parseListRulesPaginationRequest(r *http.Request) (int64, string, *apiFuncResult) {\n\tvar (\n\t\tparsedMaxGroups int64 = -1\n\t\terr             error\n\t)\n\tmaxGroups := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_limit\")\n\tnextToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"group_next_token\")\n\n\tif nextToken != \"\" && maxGroups == \"\" {\n\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be present in order to paginate over the groups\"), \"group_next_token\")\n\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t}\n\n\tif maxGroups != \"\" {\n\t\tparsedMaxGroups, err = strconv.ParseInt(maxGroups, 10, 32)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(fmt.Errorf(\"group_limit needs to be a valid number: %w\", err), \"group_limit\")\n\t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parsedMaxGroups <= 0 {\n\t\t\terrResult := invalidParamError(errors.New(\"group_limit needs to be greater than 0\"), \"group_limit\")\n\t\t\treturn -1, \"\", &errResult\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif parsedMaxGroups > 0 {\n\t\treturn parsedMaxGroups, nextToken, nil\n\t}\n\n\treturn -1, \"\", nil\n}\n\nfunc getRuleGroupNextToken(file, group string) string {\n\th := sha1.New()\n\th.Write([]byte(file + \";\" + group))\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(h.Sum(nil))\n}\n\ntype prometheusConfig struct {\n\tYAML string `json:\"yaml\"`\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveRuntimeInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstatus, err := api.runtimeInfo()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{status, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{status, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveBuildInfo(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{api.buildInfo, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveConfig(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tcfg := &prometheusConfig{\n\t\tYAML: api.config().String(),\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{cfg, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveFlags(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\treturn apiFuncResult{api.flagsMap, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\n// TSDBStat holds the information about individual cardinality.\ntype TSDBStat struct {\n\tName  string `json:\"name\"`\n\tValue uint64 `json:\"value\"`\n}\n\n// HeadStats has information about the TSDB head.\ntype HeadStats struct {\n\tNumSeries     uint64 `json:\"numSeries\"`\n\tNumLabelPairs int    `json:\"numLabelPairs\"`\n\tChunkCount    int64  `json:\"chunkCount\"`\n\tMinTime       int64  `json:\"minTime\"`\n\tMaxTime       int64  `json:\"maxTime\"`\n}\n\n// TSDBStatus has information of cardinality statistics from postings.\ntype TSDBStatus struct {\n\tHeadStats                   HeadStats  `json:\"headStats\"`\n\tSeriesCountByMetricName     []TSDBStat `json:\"seriesCountByMetricName\"`\n\tLabelValueCountByLabelName  []TSDBStat `json:\"labelValueCountByLabelName\"`\n\tMemoryInBytesByLabelName    []TSDBStat `json:\"memoryInBytesByLabelName\"`\n\tSeriesCountByLabelValuePair []TSDBStat `json:\"seriesCountByLabelValuePair\"`\n}\n\n// TSDBStatsFromIndexStats converts a index.Stat slice to a TSDBStat slice.\nfunc TSDBStatsFromIndexStats(stats []index.Stat) []TSDBStat {\n\tresult := make([]TSDBStat, 0, len(stats))\n\tfor _, item := range stats {\n\t\titem := TSDBStat{Name: item.Name, Value: item.Count}\n\t\tresult = append(result, item)\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveTSDBBlocks(*http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tblockMetas, err := api.db.BlockMetas()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting block metadata: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{\n\t\tdata: map[string][]tsdb.BlockMeta{\n\t\t\t\"blocks\": blockMetas,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) serveTSDBStatus(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit := 10\n\tif s := r.FormValue(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tif limit, err = strconv.Atoi(s); err != nil || limit < 1 {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, errors.New(\"limit must be a positive number\")}, nil, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\ts, err := api.db.Stats(labels.MetricName, limit)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tmetrics, err := api.gatherer.Gather()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorInternal, fmt.Errorf(\"error gathering runtime status: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tchunkCount := int64(math.NaN())\n\tfor _, mF := range metrics {\n\t\tif *mF.Name == \"prometheus_tsdb_head_chunks\" {\n\t\t\tm := mF.Metric[0]\n\t\t\tif m.Gauge != nil {\n\t\t\t\tchunkCount = {\n\t\ts, ns := math.Modf(t)\n\t\tns = math.Round(ns*1000) / 1000\n\t\treturn time.Unix(int64(s), int64(ns*float64(time.Second))).UTC(), nil\n\t}\n\tif t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s); err It overflows int64\", s)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn time.Duration(ts), nil\n\t}\n\tif d, err := model.ParseDuration(s); err == nil {\n\t\treturn time.Duration(d), nil\n\t}\n\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"cannot parse %q to a valid duration\", s)\n}\n\nfunc parseMatchersParam(matchers []string) ([][]*labels.Matcher, error) {\n\tmatcherSets, err := parser.ParseMetricSelectors(matchers)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\nOUTER:\n\tfor _, ms := range matcherSets {\n\t\tfor _, lm := range ms {\n\t\t\tif lm != nil && !lm.Matches(\"\") {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue OUTER\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"match[] must contain at least one non-empty matcher\")\n\t}\n\treturn matcherSets, nil\n}\n\n// parseLimitParam returning 0 means no limit is to be applied.\nfunc parseLimitParam(limitStr string) (limit int, err error) {\n\tif limitStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn limit, nil\n\t}\n\n\tlimit, err = strconv.Atoi(limitStr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn limit, err\n\t}\n\tif limit < 0 {\n\t\treturn limit, errors.New(\"limit must be non-negative\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn limit, nil\n}\n\n// toHintLimit increases the API limit, as returned by parseLimitParam, by 1.\n// This allows for emitting warnings when the results are truncated.\nfunc toHintLimit(limit int) int {\n\t// 0 means no limit and avoid int overflow\n\tif limit > 0 && limit < math.MaxInt {\n\t\treturn limit + 1\n\t}\n\treturn limit\n}\n\n// truncateResults truncates result for queryRange() and query().\n// No truncation for other types(Scalars or Strings).\nfunc truncateResults(result *promql.Result, limit int) (*promql.Result, bool) {\n\tisTruncated := false\n\n\tswitch v := result.Value.(type) {\n\tcase promql.Matrix:\n\t\tif len(v) > limit {\n\t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n\t\t\tisTruncated = true\n\t\t}\n\tcase promql.Vector:\n\t\tif len(v) > limit {\n\t\t\tresult.Value = v[:limit]\n\t\t\tisTruncated = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Return the modified result. nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) query(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\tts, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"time\", api.now())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"time\")\n\t}\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithDeadline(ctx, api.now().Add(timeout))\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewInstantQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), ts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) formatQuery(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{expr.Pretty(0), nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (*API) parseQuery(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{data: translateAST(expr), err: nil, warnings: nil, finalizer: nil}\n}\n\nfunc extractQueryOpts(r *http.Request) (promql.QueryOpts, error) {\n\tvar duration time.Duration\n\n\tif strDuration := r.FormValue(\"lookback_delta\"); strDuration != \"\" {\n\t\tparsedDuration, err := parseDuration(strDuration)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing lookback delta duration: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tduration = parsedDuration\n\t}\n\n\treturn promql.NewPrometheusQueryOpts(r.FormValue(\"stats\") == \"all\", duration), nil\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryRange(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\tstart, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"start\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTime(r.FormValue(\"end\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif end.Before(start) {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start time\"), \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tstep, err := parseDuration(r.FormValue(\"step\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\tif step <= 0 {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(errors.New(\"zero or negative query resolution step widths are not accepted. Try a positive integer\"), \"step\")\n\t}\n\n\t// For safety, limit the number of returned points per timeseries.\n\t// This is sufficient for 60s resolution for a week or 1h resolution for a year.\n\tif end.Sub(start)/step > 11000 {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"exceeded maximum resolution of 11,000 points per timeseries. Try decreasing the query resolution (?step=XX)\")\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tif to := r.FormValue(\"timeout\"); to != \"\" {\n\t\tvar cancel context.CancelFunc\n\t\ttimeout, err := parseDuration(to)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"timeout\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, timeout)\n\t\tdefer cancel()\n\t}\n\n\topts, err := extractQueryOpts(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tqry, err := api.QueryEngine.NewRangeQuery(ctx, api.Queryable, opts, r.FormValue(\"query\"), start, end, step)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"query\")\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call qry.Close ourselves (which is\n\t// required in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tqry.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tctx = httputil.ContextFromRequest(ctx, r)\n\n\tres := qry.Exec(ctx)\n\tif res.Err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(res.Err), res.Warnings, qry.Close}\n\t}\n\n\twarnings := res.Warnings\n\tif limit > 0 {\n\t\tvar isTruncated bool\n\n\t\tres, isTruncated = truncateResults(res, limit)\n\t\tif isTruncated {\n\t\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Optional stats field in response if parameter \"stats\" is not empty.\n\tsr := api.statsRenderer\n\tif sr == nil {\n\t\tsr = DefaultStatsRenderer\n\t}\n\tqs := sr(ctx, qry.Stats(), r.FormValue(\"stats\"))\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{&QueryData{\n\t\tResultType: res.Value.Type(),\n\t\tResult:     res.Value,\n\t\tStats:      qs,\n\t}, nil, warnings, qry.Close}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) queryExemplars(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\tif end.Before(start) {\n\t\terr := errors.New(\"end timestamp must not be before start timestamp\")\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\texpr, err := parser.ParseExpr(r.FormValue(\"query\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tselectors := parser.ExtractSelectors(expr)\n\tif len(selectors) < 1 {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, nil, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\teq, err := api.ExemplarQueryable.ExemplarQuerier(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tres, err := eq.Select(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end), selectors...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{res, nil, nil, nil}\n}\n\nfunc returnAPIError(err error) *apiError {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar eqc promql.ErrQueryCanceled\n\tvar eqt promql.ErrQueryTimeout\n\tvar es promql.ErrStorage\n\tswitch {\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqc):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &eqt):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorTimeout, err}\n\tcase errors.As(err, &es):\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorInternal, err}\n\t}\n\n\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\n\t\treturn &apiError{errorCanceled, err}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &apiError{errorExec, err}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelNames(r *http.Request) apiFuncResult {\n\tlimit, err := parseLimitParam(r.FormValue(\"limit\"))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"limit\")\n\t}\n\n\tstart, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"start\", MinTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, returnAPIError(err), nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tdefer q.Close()\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tnames    []string\n\t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t)\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tlabelNamesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n\n\t\tfor _, nil}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\twarnings.Merge(callWarnings)\n\t\t\tfor _, val := range vals {\n\t\t\t\tlabelNamesSet[val] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Convert the map to an array.\n\t\tnames = make([]string, 0, len(labelNamesSet))\n\t\tfor key := range labelNamesSet {\n\t\t\tnames = append(names, key)\n\t\t}\n\t\tslices.Sort(names)\n\t} else {\n\t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n\t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n\t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n\t\t}\n\t\tnames, warnings, err = q.LabelNames(r.Context(), hints, matchers...)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, nil}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif names == nil {\n\t\tnames = []string{}\n\t}\n\n\tif limit > 0 && len(names) > limit {\n\t\tnames = names[:limit]\n\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t}\n\treturn apiFuncResult{names, nil, warnings, nil}\n}\n\nfunc (api *API) labelValues(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tname := route.Param(ctx, \"name\")\n\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(name, \"U__\") {\n\t\tname = model.UnescapeName(name, model.ValueEncodingEscaping)\n\t}\n\n\tif !model.UTF8Validation.IsValidLabelName(name) {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid label name: %q\", name)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\n\tlimit, err := toHintLimit(limit),\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start), timestamp.FromTime(end))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\t// From now on, we must only return with a finalizer in the result (to\n\t// be called by the caller) or call q.Close ourselves (which is required\n\t// in the case of a panic).\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif result.finalizer == nil {\n\t\t\tq.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\tcloser := func() {\n\t\tq.Close()\n\t}\n\n\tvar (\n\t\tvals     []string\n\t\twarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t)\n\tif len(matcherSets) > 1 {\n\t\tvar callWarnings annotations.Annotations\n\t\tlabelValuesSet := make(map[string]struct{})\n\t\tfor _, matchers := range matcherSets {\n\t\t\tvals, callWarnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, hints, matchers...)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, closer}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\twarnings.Merge(callWarnings)\n\t\t\tfor _, val := range vals {\n\t\t\t\tlabelValuesSet[val] = struct{}{}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvals = make([]string, 0, len(labelValuesSet))\n\t\tfor val := range labelValuesSet {\n\t\t\tvals = append(vals, val)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tvar matchers []*labels.Matcher\n\t\tif len(matcherSets) == 1 {\n\t\t\tmatchers = matcherSets[0]\n\t\t}\n\t\tvals, warnings, err = q.LabelValues(ctx, name, hints, matchers...)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorExec, err}, warnings, closer}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif vals == nil {\n\t\t\tvals = []string{}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tslices.Sort(vals)\n\n\tif limit > 0 && len(vals) > limit {\n\t\tvals = vals[:limit]\n\t\twarnings = warnings.Add(errors.New(\"results truncated due to limit\"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn apiFuncResult{vals, nil, warnings, closer}\n}\n\nvar (\n\t// MinTime is the default timestamp used for the start of optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a higher value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still low enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMinTime = time.Unix(math.MinInt64/1000+62135596801, 0).UTC()\n\n\t// MaxTime is the default timestamp used for the end of optional time ranges.\n\t// Exposed to let downstream projects to reference it.\n\t//\n\t// Historical note: This should just be time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000, 0).UTC(),\n\t// but it was set to a lower value in the past due to a misunderstanding.\n\t// The value is still high enough for practical purposes, so we don't want\n\t// to change it now, avoiding confusion for importers of this variable.\n\tMaxTime = time.Unix(math.MaxInt64/1000-62135596801, 999999999).UTC()\n\n\tminTimeFormatted = MinTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\tmaxTimeFormatted = MaxTime.Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n)\n\nfunc (api *API) series(r *http.Request) (result apiFuncResult) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tif err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn apiFuncResult{nil, &apiError{errorBadData, fmt.Errorf(\"error parsing form values: %w\", err)}, nil, nil}\n\t}\n\tif invalidParamError(err, \"start\")\n\t}\n\tend, err := parseTimeParam(r, \"end\", MaxTime)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"end\")\n\t}\n\n\tmatcherSets, err := parseMatchersParam(r.Form[\"match[]\"])\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn invalidParamError(err, \"match[]\")\n\t}\n\n\tq, err := api.Queryable.Querier(timestamp.FromTime(start),
